Contact Set (who the action geometricall: ONLY the unit being aimed at — nothing else on the way is touched. (How it gets there — instant, projectile, beam — is the Action's job, not the collision's.)
Actions using it: Auto-Attack, Homing Projectile, Current Target Laser, Spawn At Target
Example: Kayle's auto-attack; Poppy / Cassiopeia / Samira's homing projectiles; Lux's locked beam; Azir's Sand Soldier. Taliyah's active too - her hitbox spawns ON the target, so nothing crosses the gap and nothing in between can be touched.

Contact Set (who the action geometricall: The FIRST unit the projectile collides with — may not be the unit aimed at.
Example: Projectile stops on the first body it meets. (taxonomy — no Set 9 unit here uses it)

Contact Set (who the action geometricall: EVERY unit along the path; the action passes through and does not stop.
Effect Recipient (who the effect lands o: Enemies in path  /  Allies in path
Actions using it: Pierce Projectile, Standard Laser, Laser Shot, Charge Into, Knock Back
Example: Kayle's pierce projectile hits enemies in the path; Sona's hits enemies AND buffs allies in the same path (two rows, one action); Sion charges through the cluster and stuns everyone he collides with; Irelia's Laser Shot pierces everyone in front of her; K'Sante's Knock Back is the odd one - the hitbox is the ENEMY he threw, and 'enemies in path' are whoever that flying body ploughs through.

Contact Set (who the action geometricall: Everyone inside the X-hex circle centred on the target/self hex (X = AOE (hex) column).
Example: Garen, Galio, Katarina, Sion, Jarvan damage enemies in the area; Swain's AOE also heals Self off each enemy it hit.

Contact Set (who the action geometricall: NO collision — the action projects nothing into the world. A self-cast, a reposition, or catching a projectile that is already coming back. There is nothing to collide with, so the recipient is not derived from geometry: Effect Recipient carries it.
Actions using it: Cast (self OR melee strike), Leap, Summon
Example: Kled's self-buffs; Swain's transform; Katarina's dash and Sion's / Jarvan's leap (the damage comes from the Circle AOE step that follows); Darius' and Yasuo's melee strikes - the blade projects no hitbox into the world, so there is nothing to collide with.

Contact Set (who the action geometricall: Continuation row: this effect belongs to the merged action instance above it.
Example: Kayle's MR-shred row under her Pierce Projectile; Swain's 2nd AOE effect row.

Contact Set (who the action geometricall: The one enemy on EACH side of the caster (up to 2). Nothing between or beyond them is touched.
Example: Sett grabs one enemy from either side and slams them together; if only one side is occupied, only that one is grabbed - and the effect is amplified by 50%.

Contact Set (who the action geometricall: The caster itself. Something WAS projected into the world and the unit it collides with on the way back is the caster.
Example: Poppy's thrown shield flies back and collides with her, applying the shield. Distinct from None: None means nothing was projected at all (a self-cast, a leap).

Collision: Collision describes GEOMETRY — the set of units the action physically touches on its way out.
Effect Recipient is that set narrowed by a faction filter (enemies / allies / self).
One Collision can therefore yield different Recipients: Pierce-All -> enemies (Kayle) or allies (Sona);
Area -> enemies in area (Garen) or Self (Swain heal). That is why the Hero tab keeps BOTH columns.
Collision is only defined for actions that PROJECT something. A self-cast or a leap touches nothing,
so its Collision is None — "Self" is a recipient, not a collision, and Effect Recipient already says it.
